docs.title: ENIGMA Menopause Transition & Beyond
docs.text:
Neuroendocrinology Working Group — Analysis & Processing Guide

Version 1 — May 2026

docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
This documentation provides step-by-step instructions for sites contributing to the ENIGMA Menopause Transition & Beyond Mega-Analysis — a large-scale, multi-site study mapping brain structural changes across the menopause transition.

This guide covers:

  • How to prepare and format your demographic and clinical data
  • How to run FreeSurfer preprocessing following ENIGMA standards
  • How to perform quality control of your outputs
  • How to share your data with the analysis team

docs.title: Data Sharing Options
docs.text:
Participating sites may contribute at one of three levels:

Option Description Option 1 Existing FreeSurfer-derived measures (v7 or v8) Option 2 Local processing using our standardised pipelines Option 3 Raw structural scans (NIFTI) for centralised processing

All contributions must include phenotypic data — see Getting Started.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For questions or data transfer arrangements, contact:

  • Goretti España-Irla — goretti.espana@charite.de
  • Claudia Barth — claudia.barth@charite.de
  • General enquiries — enigma-menopause@charite.de

docs.title: Covariates CSV — Format & Example
docs.text: The Covariates.csv file contains demographic and clinical variables for all participants in your dataset. This file is required for all contributing sites regardless of data sharing option.

docs.title: Required Columns
docs.text:
These columns must be present for all participants:

Column Type Description SubjID string Unique participant identifier Age numeric Age in years Sex numeric Biological sex (1=Female, 2=Male) Site string Scanner/site identifier Dx numeric Diagnosis (1=Healthy control) MenoStatus string Menopausal status (see below) FreeSurfer_version string FreeSurfer version used (e.g. 7.4.1)

docs.title: Menopausal Status Values
docs.text:
Use the following values for the MenoStatus column:

Value Description pre Premenopausal peri_early Early perimenopausal peri_late Late perimenopausal post_early Early postmenopausal post_late Late postmenopausal unknown Status unknown

docs.title: Optional Columns
docs.text:
Include as many of the following as available:

Column Type Description MenoType string Type of menopause (natural, surgical, chemical) AgeMenopause numeric Age at menopause (if postmenopausal) HRT_ever numeric Ever used HRT (0=No, 1=Yes) HRT_current numeric Currently using HRT (0=No, 1=Yes) HC_ever numeric Ever used hormonal contraceptives (0=No, 1=Yes) BMI numeric Body mass index (kg/m²) Handedness string Handedness (right, left, ambidextrous) Education numeric Years of education MRS_total numeric Menopause Rating Scale total score Greene_total numeric Greene Climacteric Scale total score MENQOL_total numeric MENQOL total score

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
!!! warning - Do not include any identifying information (names, dates of birth, full dates) - Use the exact column names as shown above - Missing values should be left empty (not filled with 0 or NA) - One row per participant

!!! tip Example files can be found in the examples/ directory of the pipeline package. Use these to validate your formatting before submission.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Welcome to the ENIGMA Menopause Transition & Beyond study!

This guide will help you get set up as a contributing site. Please read through carefully before starting any processing.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
Before you begin, make sure you have the following installed on your system:

  • Linux with bash Unix shell
  • FreeSurfer v7 or v8 — download here
  • R — download here
  • MATLAB with the Image Processing Toolbox (for LGI only)
  • ImageMagick
  • R packages: ggplot2, Routliers

docs.title: What You Need to Contribute
docs.text:
At minimum, each contributing site must provide:

  • T1-weighted MRI data (or existing FreeSurfer outputs)
  • A completed Covariates.csv file with demographic and clinical variables
  • Age, biological sex, intracranial volume, and scanner/site information

See the Examples section for a template.

docs.title: Choose Your Data Sharing Option
docs.text: Option What you contribute Who does the processing Option 1 Existing FreeSurfer outputs (v7/v8) You (already done) Option 2 FreeSurfer outputs using our scripts You (using our pipeline) Option 3 Raw NIFTI scans Us (centralised processing)

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Fill in your Covariates.csv — see Examples
  2. Follow the Preprocessing Steps
  3. Perform Quality Control
  4. Contact us to arrange data transfer

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
• Goretti España-Irla — goretti.espana@charite.de
  • Claudia Barth — claudia.barth@charite.de
  • General — enigma-menopause@charite.de

docs.title: Preprocessing Overview
docs.text: This section describes the neuroimaging preprocessing pipeline for the ENIGMA Menopause Transition & Beyond study.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text:
The goal of preprocessing is to extract surface-based morphometric measures from T1-weighted MRI scans using FreeSurfer. These measures include:

  • Cortical thickness
  • Cortical surface area
  • Cortical volume
  • Local Gyrification Index (LGI)
  • Subcortical volumes
  • Intracranial Volume (ICV / sbTIV)

All measures are extracted using the Desikan-Killiany atlas.

docs.title: Output Files
docs.text: File Description lh.thickness.aparc.csv Left hemisphere cortical thickness rh.thickness.aparc.csv Right hemisphere cortical thickness lh.area.aparc.csv Left hemisphere surface area rh.area.aparc.csv Right hemisphere surface area lh.volume.aparc.csv Left hemisphere cortical volume rh.volume.aparc.csv Right hemisphere cortical volume lh.lgi.aparc.csv Left hemisphere LGI rh.lgi.aparc.csv Right hemisphere LGI sbTIV.csv Intracranial volume

docs.title: Pipeline Steps
docs.text:
1. Step 1 — Run FreeSurfer recon-all
  2. Step 2 — Extract surface measures
  3. Step 3 — Extract ICV (sbTIV)
  4. Step 4 — Quality Control

See Preprocessing Steps for detailed instruct cat > docs/preprocessing/steps.md << 'EOF'

docs.title: Preprocessing Steps
docs.text: Follow these steps carefully. If you already have FreeSurfer outputs (Option 1), skip to Step 2.

docs.title: Step 1 — Run FreeSurfer recon-all
docs.text:
Run recon-all for each participant:

recon-all -s <subject_id> -i <path_to_T1.nii> -all

For 3T scanners, add the -3T flag:

recon-all -s <subject_id> -i <path_to_T1.nii> -all -3T

!!! note Use FreeSurfer v7 or v8. Note which version you used — you will need to report this in your Covariates.csv.

!!! warning Do not mix FreeSurfer v7 and v8 outputs within the same dataset.

docs.title: Step 2 — Extract Surface Measures
docs.text:
Once recon-all has finished, extract cortical measures using the ENIGMA extraction scripts.

Download the ENIGMA cortical extraction scripts:

# Contact enigma-menopause@charite.de to receive the scripts

Run the extraction:

bash extract_cortical_measures.sh <SUBJECTS_DIR>

This will generate the following files in your output/ directory:

  • lh.thickness.aparc.csv
  • rh.thickness.aparc.csv
  • lh.area.aparc.csv
  • rh.area.aparc.csv
  • lh.volume.aparc.csv
  • rh.volume.aparc.csv
  • lh.lgi.aparc.csv
  • rh.lgi.aparc.csv

docs.title: Step 3 — Extract ICV (sbTIV)
docs.text:
Run the ICV extraction script:

bash extract_ICV.sh <SUBJECTS_DIR>

This will generate sbTIV.csv in your output/ directory.

docs.title: Step 4 — Prepare Covariates
docs.text:
Fill in the Covariates.csv file with demographic and clinical variables for all participants.

See the Covariates Example for the required format and column names.

Required columns:

Column Description SubjID Unique participant identifier Age Age in years Sex Biological sex (1=Female, 2=Male) Site Scanner/site identifier Dx Diagnosis (1=Control) MenoStatus Menopausal status

docs.title: Step 5 — Quality Control
docs.text:
Before sending your data, perform visual QC on all FreeSurfer outputs.

See Visual Inspection for instructions.

docs.title: Data Transfer
docs.text:
Once preprocessing and QC are complete, compress your output files:

tar -czf <site_name>_enigma_menopause.tar.gz docs/ output/

Then contact us to arrange secure data transfer:

  • Goretti España-Irla — goretti.espana@charite.de
  • Claudia Barth — claudia.barth@charite.de

docs.title: Visual Inspection & Quality Control
docs.text: Quality control (QC) is a critical step before sharing your data. Please carefully inspect all FreeSurfer outputs before submission.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Make sure you have the following installed:

  • ImageMagick
  • R with packages: ggplot2, Routliers

docs.title: Step 1 — Generate QC Images
docs.text:
Run the ENIGMA QC script to generate surface snapshots for all participants:

bash generate_qc_images.sh <SUBJECTS_DIR>

This will create a qc/ folder with PNG images for each participant showing:

  • Pial surface (lateral and medial views)
  • White matter surface
  • Cortical parcellation overlay

docs.title: Step 2 — Visual Inspection
docs.text:
Open each image and check for the following common issues:

!!! warning "Fail — Exclude this participant" - Large holes or missing patches in the cortical surface - Severe motion artefacts in the original scan - Skull stripping failures (brain tissue removed or non-brain tissue included) - Wrong hemisphere labelling

!!! note "Flag — Use with caution" - Minor surface reconstruction errors in non-critical regions - Small dura inclusions - Mild motion artefacts

!!! success "Pass" - Clean pial and white matter surfaces - Accurate skull stripping - Correct parcellation overlay

docs.title: Step 3 — Record QC Ratings
docs.text:
Record your QC ratings for each participant in a file called qc_ratings.csv with the following format:

SubjID QC_rating QC_notes sub-001 pass sub-002 fail severe motion artefacts sub-003 flag minor dura inclusion left frontal

Include this file in your submission along with your output files.

docs.title: Step 4 — Outlier Detection
docs.text:
Run the ENIGMA outlier detection script in R:

Rscript detect_outliers.R output/

This will flag statistical outliers across cortical measures using the Routliers package. Review flagged participants and add notes to your qc_ratings.csv.

!!! tip When in doubt, flag rather than fail. We will review borderline cases together before exclusion.

docs.title: Scripts
docs.text:
This section provides an overview of the scripts you will need to run locally to extract and prepare your data for submission.

All scripts will be shared upon confirmation of participation. Contact enigma-menopause@charite.de to receive the pipeline package.

docs.title: Extraction Scripts
docs.text: Script Language Description extract_cortical_measures.sh Bash Extracts cortical thickness, area, volume and LGI from FreeSurfer outputs extract_ICV.sh Bash Extracts intracranial volume (sbTIV) generate_qc_images.sh Bash Generates PNG snapshots for visual QC detect_outliers.R R Flags statistical outliers across cortical measures

docs.title: Bash scripts
docs.text:
• FreeSurfer v7 or v8
  • ImageMagick

docs.title: R scripts
docs.text:
• R (v4.0 or higher)
  • Packages: ggplot2, Routliers
install.packages(c("ggplot2", "Routliers"))

!!! note Always use the latest version of the scripts provided by the analysis team. Do not modify the scripts unless instructed.
